title: malvastrum dissectum var. cockerellii
intro: Malvastrum dissectum var. cockerellii, also known as the Cockrell's malvastrum or desert holly, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its roots, which have been used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Malvastrum dissectum var. cockerellii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of wounds, infections, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant's roots can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to promote wound healing.
